_notes: Ten questions used to evaluate end-to-end QA accuracy. Mix of single-hop, multi-hop, and aggregation questions. Each has a short canonical answer plus the source articles a correct answer should rely on. Phrasing is intentionally varied — some terse, some verbose — to catch surface-similarity retrieval failures.

questions.question: What drugs treat epithelioid sarcoma and target the EZH2 gene product?
What was the original tradename of trastuzumab when it was first approved?
Imatinib is used to treat which leukemia, and what fusion protein does it inhibit?
Which is more selective for EZH2 over EZH1 — GSK126 or EPZ005687?
questions.answer: GSK126 is more selective (150-fold over EZH1, vs 50-fold for EPZ005687).

questions.question: Which tumour-suppressor gene encodes a protein commonly called 'the guardian of the genome'?
Mutations in which gene predispose women to a much higher lifetime risk of breast cancer, and what type of protein does it encode?
questions.answer: BRCA1; a tumour suppressor / DNA repair protein

questions.question: List at least three EZH2 inhibitors mentioned in the corpus.
questions.answer: Any three of: DZNep, EPZ005687, EI1, GSK126, UNC1999, tazemetostat

questions.question: Pembrolizumab is a checkpoint inhibitor — which immune-checkpoint receptor does it target, and which skin cancer is it commonly used to treat?
questions.answer: Targets PD-1; used to treat melanoma (also other cancers)

questions.question: Apoptosis is regulated by two main protein families — name them and say briefly what each does.
questions.answer: Caspases (proteases that execute the cell-death programme) and the Bcl-2 family (anti- and pro-apoptotic regulators of mitochondrial outer-membrane permeabilisation).
